**task_prompt**: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis

• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram

• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider
Please design a process according to the task and output it in the following format:

When the process starts, please output '<process>'
An activity is represented as follows:
<activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
The exclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<exclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</exclusiveGateway>
The parallel gateway is represented as follows:
<parallelGateway id=''>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</parallelGateway>
The inclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<inclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</inclusiveGateway>
At the end of the process, please output '</process>'

There are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:
    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.
    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.
    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.
    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute. When the branch unconditional within the exclusive gateway, the condition attribute of the branch =''. However, all branches within a exclusive gateway can only be conditional, or both unconditional.
    5) The branch in the parallel gateway has no condition attribute.
    6) Each branch in the inclusive gateway must have condition.
    7) There must be at least one activity inside the branch tag.
    8) The activity of a sequential relation need not be represented by a gateway, that is, there is no 'sequenceGateway' label.

Here are some examples of process text, and you can refer to these process texts but cannot copy them:
example1(Club Application Process):
<process>
  <activity role='students' action='fill out the Association application form' objects='' id='activity1'/>
  <activity role='club' action='Eligibility Checks' objects='Students club Application Form' id='activity2'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='Approved' id='branch1'>
      <activity role='' action='' objects='' id='activity3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Not approved' id='branch2'>
      <activity role='students' action='modify application form' objects='Student club Application Form' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
  <parallelGateway  id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='students' action='club interview' objects='' id='activity5'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='students' action='paying the interview fee' objects='interview fee' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
  <activity role='club' action='Rating' objects='Student interview results' id='activity7'/>
  <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='score>=90' id='branch5'>
      <activity role='Club and students' action='club orientation party' objects='cakes,gifts' id='activity8'/>
      <activity role='students' action='participating in club activities' objects='' id='activity9'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='score>=80' id='branch6'>
      <activity role='students' action='get the outstanding student award' objects='prize' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='score<90' id='branch7'>
      <activity role='students' action='failure to join the club' objects='' id='activity11'/>
    </branch>
  </inclusiveGateway>
</process>
example2(Shipping process):
<process>
  <activity role='sender' action='Prepare to send a package' objects=' package' id='activity1'/>
  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='select pick-up' id='branch1'>
      <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
        <branch id='branch2'>
          <activity role='Sender' action='Select Courier pick-up location' objects='package' id='activity2'/>
        </branch>
        <branch id='branch3'>
          <activity role='Express delivery company' action='Arrange a Courier' objects='' id='activity3'/>
        </branch>
      </parallelGateway>
      <activity role='Courier' action='receiving package' objects='package' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='choose to send by yourself' id='branch4'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
        <branch id='branch4'>
          <activity role='Sender' action='go to the mailing point to send' objects='package,mailing point address' id='activity5'/>
        </branch>
        <branch id='branch5'>
          <activity role='Sender' action='Other ways' objects='package' id='activity6'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>
example3(Medical Process):
<process>
  <activity role='medical staff' action='Preliminary Check' objects='' id='activity1'/>
  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='medical staff' action='Assessment C' objects='' id='activity2'/>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
        <branch condition='If X more than 10' id='branch2'>
          <activity role='medical staff' action='Procedure A' objects='' id='activity3'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='If X between 5 to 10' id='branch3'>
          <activity role='medical staff' action='Procedure B' objects='' id='activity4'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='Otherwise' id='branch4'>
          <activity role='medical staff' action='Skip' objects='' id='activity5'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='medical staff' action='Notify Relevant Team' objects='' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <activity role='medical staff' action='Monitor Variable Y at Regular Intervals' objects='' id='activity7'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch7'>
      <activity role='medical staff' action='Procedure D' objects='' id='activity8'/>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
        <branch condition='If not contradicted' id='branch8'>
          <activity role='medical staff' action='Relocate Entity' objects='' id='activity9'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='If contradicted' id='branch9'>
          <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway_sub'>
            <branch condition='If condition G was satisfied' id='branch_sub1'>
              <activity role='medical staff' action='Perform option A' objects='' id='activity10'/>
            </branch>
            <branch condition='If condition H was satisfied' id='branch_sub2'>
              <activity role='medical staff' action='Perform option B' objects='' id='activity11'/>
            </branch>
          </inclusiveGateway>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
        <branch condition='If No Prior Record' id='branch11'>
          <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
            <branch condition='Option Alpha' id='branch12'>
              <activity role='medical staff' action='Open new record using Alpha' objects='' id='activity12'/>
            </branch>
            <branch condition='Option Beta' id='branch13'>
              <activity role='medical staff' action='Open new record using Beta' objects='' id='activity13'/>
            </branch>
          </inclusiveGateway>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='If Prior Record Exists' id='branch14'>
          <activity role='medical staff' action='Skip' objects='' id='activity14'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>
</process>
You must follow the bellow notes:
Note 1: You must include all tasks mentioned in the QBP's recommended practices section, regardless of perceived importance.
Note 2: Retain important information such as timeframe and frequency details within activity labels.
Note 3: As these are clinical pathways, use concurrency when there is no obvious sequence between activities.

[2026-21-02 23:23:51 INFO] Process design expert: **Process design expert<->Team leader on : ProcessGeneration, turn 0**

[You're a team leader. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.
Your main responsibilities will include guiding the process design expert through process generation, process refinement, and so on.
Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

<process>
  <activity role='ED triage nurse' action='identify suspected acute heart failure presentation and initiate episode-of-care pathway' objects='patient, triage information' id='activity1'/>
  <activity role='ED physician' action='perform risk assessment/stratification using a standardized multi-condition tool (consistently applied across hospitals)' objects='standardized risk stratification tool, patient history and exam' id='activity2'/>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' id='activity3'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='troponin measurements' id='activity5'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='troponin measurements' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='complete blood count' id='activity7'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='complete blood count' id='activity8'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='electrocardiogram' id='activity9'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='interpret electrocardiogram' objects='electrocardiogram results' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='radiology' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity11'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='review chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray report/images' id='activity12'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='frequently measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs monitor, oxygen saturation monitor' id='activity13'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch7'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='administer furosemide in ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis (urine output/symptoms/weight if available)' objects='furosemide, diuresis response parameters' id='activity14'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='No recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
      <activity role='cardiology/echo lab' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity15'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='review echocardiogram findings' objects='echocardiogram report' id='activity16'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch9'>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='retrieve and review recent echocardiogram' objects='prior echocardiogram report/images' id='activity17'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

  <activity role='ED physician' action='calculate acute heart failure risk score (e.g., EHMRG) to support clinical decision-making and predict 7-day mortality risk' objects='EHMRG inputs, lab/imaging/vital sign data' id='activity18'/>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway2'>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for respiratory distress' objects='respiratory assessment' id='activity19'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch11'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for hypoxemia' objects='oxygen saturation readings/ABG if available' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch12'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess severity of pulmonary edema' objects='clinical exam, chest x-ray findings' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch13'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess response to furosemide administered in ED (poor response as high-risk marker)' objects='urine output, symptoms, vital signs' id='activity22'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch14'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for hemodynamic compromise' objects='blood pressure, perfusion markers' id='activity23'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch15'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for significant arrhythmias' objects='ECG/telemetry' id='activity24'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch16'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for positive troponin' objects='troponin results' id='activity25'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='respiratory distress present' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate higher level provider/critical care support for respiratory distress' objects='consult request' id='activity26'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='hypoxemia present' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='initiate escalation pathway for hypoxemia (consider noninvasive or invasive ventilation)' objects='oxygen/ventilation resources' id='activity27'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='severe pulmonary edema present' id='branch19'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='escalate care for severe pulmonary edema and coordinate higher-acuity monitoring' objects='care escalation plan' id='activity28'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='poor response to furosemide in ED' id='branch20'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='escalate to higher-acuity evaluation for diuretic nonresponse' objects='diuresis response data' id='activity29'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='hemodynamic compromise present' id='branch21'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate critical care/cardiology for hemodynamic compromise' objects='hemodynamic data, consult request' id='activity30'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='significant arrhythmias present' id='branch22'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='request urgent cardiology support for significant arrhythmias' objects='ECG/telemetry data, consult request' id='activity31'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='troponin positive' id='branch23'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate pathway for myocardial injury/possible ischemia and involve cardiology' objects='troponin results, ECG data' id='activity32'/>
    </branch>
  </inclusiveGateway>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway3'>
    <branch id='branch24'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='confirm patient has responded to initial treatment in the ED (required for discharge consideration)' objects='clinical response, vital signs, diuresis response' id='activity33'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch25'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess other discharge considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy)' objects='goals-of-care documentation, cognitive status, prognosis factors' id='activity34'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='Low intensity: EHMRG quintiles 1–2 AND responded to initial ED treatment AND no other considerations' id='branch26'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='treat in ED or outpatient setting and discharge home without inpatient admission' objects='discharge plan' id='activity35'/>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of ED discharge' objects='appointment request, discharge summary' id='activity36'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Average intensity: requires inpatient admission with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch27'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='admission orders, handover' id='activity37'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='High intensity: requires ventilation (noninvasive or invasive) and/or ICU admission with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch28'>
      <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='initiate ventilation as indicated (noninvasive or invasive) and admit to ICU with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ventilation equipment, ICU bed request' id='activity38'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility required (transfer pathway invoked)' id='branch29'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate hospital pathway/mechanism to transfer patient to a higher level provider/facility' objects='transfer protocol, receiving team contact' id='activity39'/>
      <activity role='transport team' action='transfer patient to higher level provider/facility' objects='patient, transfer documentation' id='activity40'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility not required' id='branch30'>
      <activity role='receiving unit/ED team' action='continue care at assigned level (outpatient/ward/ICU) with documented risk stratification and results' objects='risk stratification summary, investigation results, care plan' id='activity41'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>

[2026-21-02 23:23:51 INFO] Team leader: **[Start Chat]**

[You're a process design expert. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.
You are familiar with exclusive gateway, parallel gateway and inclusive gateway in BPMN. Your main responsibilities include process generation, process refinement, process review and so on, guided by team leader.
You model the process of a clinical practice guideline to a clinical pathway.
Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider
process text:
<process>
  <activity role='ED triage nurse' action='identify suspected acute heart failure presentation and initiate episode-of-care pathway' objects='patient, triage information' id='activity1'/>
  <activity role='ED physician' action='perform risk assessment/stratification using a standardized multi-condition tool (consistently applied across hospitals)' objects='standardized risk stratification tool, patient history and exam' id='activity2'/>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway1'>
    <branch id='branch1'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' id='activity3'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='serum creatinine and electrolyte levels' id='activity4'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch2'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='troponin measurements' id='activity5'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='troponin measurements' id='activity6'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch3'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='obtain bloodwork' objects='complete blood count' id='activity7'/>
      <activity role='laboratory' action='run assay and report results' objects='complete blood count' id='activity8'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch4'>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='perform electrocardiogram' objects='electrocardiogram' id='activity9'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='interpret electrocardiogram' objects='electrocardiogram results' id='activity10'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch5'>
      <activity role='radiology' action='perform chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray' id='activity11'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='review chest x-ray examination' objects='chest x-ray report/images' id='activity12'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch6'>
      <activity role='ED nurse' action='frequently measure heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized' objects='vital signs monitor, oxygen saturation monitor' id='activity13'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch7'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='administer furosemide in ED and assess responsiveness to diuresis (urine output/symptoms/weight if available)' objects='furosemide, diuresis response parameters' id='activity14'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='No recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch8'>
      <activity role='cardiology/echo lab' action='perform echocardiogram' objects='echocardiogram' id='activity15'/>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='review echocardiogram findings' objects='echocardiogram report' id='activity16'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Recent echocardiogram is available' id='branch9'>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='retrieve and review recent echocardiogram' objects='prior echocardiogram report/images' id='activity17'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

  <activity role='ED physician' action='calculate acute heart failure risk score (e.g., EHMRG) to support clinical decision-making and predict 7-day mortality risk' objects='EHMRG inputs, lab/imaging/vital sign data' id='activity18'/>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway2'>
    <branch id='branch10'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for respiratory distress' objects='respiratory assessment' id='activity19'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch11'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for hypoxemia' objects='oxygen saturation readings/ABG if available' id='activity20'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch12'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess severity of pulmonary edema' objects='clinical exam, chest x-ray findings' id='activity21'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch13'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess response to furosemide administered in ED (poor response as high-risk marker)' objects='urine output, symptoms, vital signs' id='activity22'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch14'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for hemodynamic compromise' objects='blood pressure, perfusion markers' id='activity23'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch15'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for significant arrhythmias' objects='ECG/telemetry' id='activity24'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch16'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess for positive troponin' objects='troponin results' id='activity25'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <inclusiveGateway id='inclusivegateway1'>
    <branch condition='respiratory distress present' id='branch17'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate higher level provider/critical care support for respiratory distress' objects='consult request' id='activity26'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='hypoxemia present' id='branch18'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='initiate escalation pathway for hypoxemia (consider noninvasive or invasive ventilation)' objects='oxygen/ventilation resources' id='activity27'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='severe pulmonary edema present' id='branch19'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='escalate care for severe pulmonary edema and coordinate higher-acuity monitoring' objects='care escalation plan' id='activity28'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='poor response to furosemide in ED' id='branch20'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='escalate to higher-acuity evaluation for diuretic nonresponse' objects='diuresis response data' id='activity29'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='hemodynamic compromise present' id='branch21'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate critical care/cardiology for hemodynamic compromise' objects='hemodynamic data, consult request' id='activity30'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='significant arrhythmias present' id='branch22'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='request urgent cardiology support for significant arrhythmias' objects='ECG/telemetry data, consult request' id='activity31'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='troponin positive' id='branch23'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate pathway for myocardial injury/possible ischemia and involve cardiology' objects='troponin results, ECG data' id='activity32'/>
    </branch>
  </inclusiveGateway>

  <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway3'>
    <branch id='branch24'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='confirm patient has responded to initial treatment in the ED (required for discharge consideration)' objects='clinical response, vital signs, diuresis response' id='activity33'/>
    </branch>
    <branch id='branch25'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='assess other discharge considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy)' objects='goals-of-care documentation, cognitive status, prognosis factors' id='activity34'/>
    </branch>
  </parallelGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway2'>
    <branch condition='Low intensity: EHMRG quintiles 1–2 AND responded to initial ED treatment AND no other considerations' id='branch26'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='treat in ED or outpatient setting and discharge home without inpatient admission' objects='discharge plan' id='activity35'/>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='arrange follow-up visit with primary care provider within days of ED discharge' objects='appointment request, discharge summary' id='activity36'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Average intensity: requires inpatient admission with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch27'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='admit patient to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='admission orders, handover' id='activity37'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='High intensity: requires ventilation (noninvasive or invasive) and/or ICU admission with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch28'>
      <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='initiate ventilation as indicated (noninvasive or invasive) and admit to ICU with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ventilation equipment, ICU bed request' id='activity38'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility required (transfer pathway invoked)' id='branch29'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='activate hospital pathway/mechanism to transfer patient to a higher level provider/facility' objects='transfer protocol, receiving team contact' id='activity39'/>
      <activity role='transport team' action='transfer patient to higher level provider/facility' objects='patient, transfer documentation' id='activity40'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility not required' id='branch30'>
      <activity role='receiving unit/ED team' action='continue care at assigned level (outpatient/ward/ICU) with documented risk stratification and results' objects='risk stratification summary, investigation results, care plan' id='activity41'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>
Check which activities in the process text can be expanded into sub-activities and add these sub-activities to the process text. Please note:
1) Only expand important activities, not all of them.
2) Ensure that the process text remains logical when adding sub-activities.
3) Add appropriate gateways when adding activities.
4) When modifying the process text, note whether the newly added activity is a sequential, exclusive, or concurrent relationship.
5) Only expand activities if it's necessary and do not create overly detailed sub-activities.
Here are some examples of expanding activities in process texts into sub-activities. You can refer to them but cannot copy them:
1. 'payment with card/cash' activity with id='activity2' can be expanded to:
   sub-activity:'Payment with card' and 'Payment with cash' 
   activity relations: exclusive
2. 'club interview id and confimation email' activity with id='activity5' can be expanded to:
   sub-activity:'Interview at the club' and 'Send confirmation email'
   activity relations: sequential
3. 'Submit the materials' activity with id='activity9' can be expanded to:
   sub-activity:'Submit interview materials' and 'Submit examination materials' 
   activity relations: parallel
After extending the activity, number the activity or gateway in addition to the original id. If the sub-activity are exclusive or parallel relationships, you need to add them in the form of gateways when modifying the process text:

When the process starts, please output '<process>'
An activity is represented as follows:
<activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
The exclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<exclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</exclusiveGateway>
The parallel gateway is represented as follows:
<parallelGateway id=''>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</parallelGateway>
The inclusive gateway is represented as follows:
<inclusiveGateway id=''>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
  <branch condition='' id=''>
    <activity role='' action='' objects='' id=''/>
  </branch>
</inclusiveGateway>
At the end of the process, please output '</process>'

There are a few requirements that should be noted when generating process text:
    1) role, action, objects and id in an activity represent the subject of the activity, the activity behavior, the items needed to perform in the activity and the activity id, respectively.
    2) There are at least two branches within the exclusive gateway and the parallel gateway.
    3) Activity, gateway, and branch id attributes must be unique.
    4) When the branch in the exclusive gateway has a condition, fill the condition into the condition attribute. When the branch unconditional within the exclusive gateway, the condition attribute of the branch =''. However, all branches within a exclusive gateway can only be conditional, or both unconditional.
    5) The branch in the parallel gateway has no condition attribute.
    6) Each branch in the inclusive gateway must have condition.
    7) There must be at least one activity inside the branch tag.
    8) The activity of a sequential relation need not be represented by a gateway, that is, there is no 'sequenceGateway' label.

First output which activities can be extended according to the above format (...... can be expanded to:.......) and then give the modified process text. Don't say anything else.

[2026-21-02 23:24:53 INFO] Process design expert: **Process design expert<->Team leader on : ActivityRefinement, turn 0**

[You're a team leader. Now, we share a common interest in collaborating to successfully complete a task assigned by a new customer.
Your main responsibilities will include guiding the process design expert through process generation, process refinement, and so on.
Here is a new customer's task: Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure

The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above).

Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure

Module 1: Risk Stratification

Module 1 Recommended Practices

1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification
Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals.

1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis
Initial investigations should include:
• serum creatinine and electrolyte levels
• troponin measurements
• complete blood count
• electrocardiogram
• chest x-ray examination
• an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available
• frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized

1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups
Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED.
Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing.
High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing.

High-risk markers include:
• respiratory distress
• hypoxemia
• severity of pulmonary edema
• poor response to furosemide administered in ED
• hemodynamic compromise
• significant arrhythmias
• positive troponin

1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score
An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score).

As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital.

Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.

Module 1 Implementation Considerations
General Considerations for Risk Stratification
• Hospitals should use a common standardized risk stratification assessment tool or process to determine where and how to assist with clinical decision making when patients present to the emergency department.
• All hospitals should have a pathway or mechanism to transfer patients to a higher level provider.
To complete the task, I will give you one or more instructions, and you must help me to write a specific solution that appropriately solves the requested instruction based on your expertise and my needs.]

      </exclusiveGateway>

    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

      <activity role='ED physician' action='handover to inpatient team and receiving nurse' objects='handover summary, investigation results, risk score' id='activity37_3'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='High intensity: requires ventilation (noninvasive or invasive) and/or ICU admission with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' id='branch28'>
      <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='prepare for ventilatory support and airway management as indicated' objects='airway equipment, monitoring, sedation plan if needed' id='activity38_1'/>
      <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway4'>
        <branch condition='Noninvasive ventilation appropriate' id='branch31'>
          <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='initiate noninvasive ventilation' objects='NIV device, mask/interface settings' id='activity38_2'/>
        </branch>
        <branch condition='Invasive ventilation required' id='branch32'>
          <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='perform endotracheal intubation and initiate mechanical ventilation' objects='intubation kit, ventilator, RSI medications' id='activity38_3'/>
        </branch>
      </exclusiveGateway>
      <activity role='ED physician/critical care' action='request ICU bed and admit to ICU with higher nurse-to-patient staffing' objects='ICU bed request, ICU admission orders, handover' id='activity38_4'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>

  <exclusiveGateway id='exclusivegateway3'>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility required (transfer pathway invoked)' id='branch29'>
      <activity role='ED physician' action='determine required higher level of care and identify appropriate receiving facility' objects='transfer criteria, receiving facility options' id='activity39_1'/>
      <parallelGateway id='parallelgateway4'>
        <branch id='branch33'>
          <activity role='ED physician' action='contact receiving team and obtain acceptance' objects='receiving physician contact, clinical summary' id='activity39_2'/>
        </branch>
        <branch id='branch34'>
          <activity role='ED team' action='prepare transfer documentation and stabilize patient for transport' objects='transfer forms, imaging/lab reports, stabilization checklist' id='activity39_3'/>
        </branch>
      </parallelGateway>
      <activity role='ED staff' action='arrange transport resources and coordinate departure time' objects='transport request, ETA, handoff details' id='activity39_4'/>
      <activity role='transport team' action='transfer patient to higher level provider/facility' objects='patient, transfer documentation' id='activity40'/>
    </branch>
    <branch condition='Higher level provider/facility not required' id='branch30'>
      <activity role='receiving unit/ED team' action='handover to assigned care setting with documented risk stratification and results' objects='risk stratification summary, investigation results, care plan' id='activity41_1'/>
      <activity role='receiving unit/ED team' action='ensure ongoing monitoring and treatment orders are active per assigned level of care' objects='monitoring orders, medication orders, escalation criteria' id='activity41_2'/>
    </branch>
  </exclusiveGateway>
</process>

| **task_prompt** | Episode of Care for Acute Heart Failure The Acute Heart Failure Episode-of-Care Advisory Panel developed the episode-of-care model for acute heart failure (Figure 6). Modules 1 through 3 represent the acute heart failure episode of care. The following recommendations include the recommendations from the clinical handbook on acute heart failure published in 2013 and updates to the recommendations (as noted in the What’s New box above). Figure 6. Episode-of-Care Model for Acute Heart Failure Module 1: Risk Stratification Module 1 Recommended Practices 1.1 Risk Assessment/Stratification Risk-stratification tools that can be used for multiple conditions (including HF) should be developed and consistently applied across all Ontario hospitals. 1.2 Responsiveness to Diuresis Initial investigations should include: • serum creatinine and electrolyte levels • troponin measurements • complete blood count • electrocardiogram • chest x-ray examination • an echocardiogram if no recent echocardiogram is available • frequent measurement of heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation until patient is stabilized 1.3 Risk Stratification Patient Groups Low intensity: Patients can be treated in the ED or in outpatient settings and discharged home without requiring inpatient admission. However, these patients require a follow-up visit with their primary care provider within days of discharge from the ED. Average intensity: Patients require admission to inpatient care with normal nurse-to-patient staffing. High intensity: Patients require ventilation (either noninvasive or invasive ventilation) and/or admission to an intensive care unit with higher nurse-to-patient staffing. High-risk markers include: • respiratory distress • hypoxemia • severity of pulmonary edema • poor response to furosemide administered in ED • hemodynamic compromise • significant arrhythmias • positive troponin 1.4 Heart Failure Risk Score An acute heart failure risk score—for example, the EHMRG—be calculated to assist with clinical decision-making and predicting the 7-day mortality risk of HF patients (predicted mortality risk increases incrementally with higher EHMRG risk score). As a general guide, patients who are low-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 1 and 2) can be considered for discharge home if they have responded to initial treatment in the ED, provided that there are no other considerations (e.g., advanced-directives, severe dementia, estimated impact of admission on life-expectancy, etc.). Patients who are at higher-risk (e.g., EHMRG quintiles 3-5) should be admitted to hospital. Abbreviations: ACCF, American College of Cardiology Foundation; AHA, American Heart Association; CCS, Canadian Cardiovascular Society; ED, emergency department; EHMRG, Emergency Heart Failure Mortality Risk Grade; HF, heart failure; NICE, National Institute for Clinical Excellence.
